# Module description

This module provides a way for securely storing passwords and other secrets.

It uses D-Bus [Secret Service] API that is supported by GNOME Keyring,
KWallet (since version 5.97) and KeePassXC.

The main classes provided are `secretstorage.Item`, representing a secret
item (that has a *label*, a *secret* and some *attributes*) and
`secretstorage.Collection`, a place items are stored in.

SecretStorage supports most of the functions provided by Secret Service,
including creating and deleting items and collections, editing items,
locking and unlocking collections (asynchronous unlocking is also supported).

The documentation can be found on [secretstorage.readthedocs.io].

# Building the module

:::{note}
SecretStorage 3.x supports Python 3.6 and newer versions.
If you have an older version of Python, install SecretStorage 2.x:

```
pip install "SecretStorage < 3"
```
:::

SecretStorage requires these packages to work:

- [Jeepney]
- [python-cryptography]

To build SecretStorage, use this command:

```
python3 setup.py build
```

If you have [Sphinx] installed, you can also build the documentation:

```
python3 setup.py build_sphinx
```

# Testing the module

First, make sure that you have the Secret Service daemon installed.
The [GNOME Keyring] is the reference server-side implementation for the
Secret Service specification.

Then, start the daemon and unlock the `default` collection, if needed.
The testsuite will fail to run if the `default` collection exists and is
locked. If it does not exist, the testsuite can also use the temporary
`session` collection, as provided by the GNOME Keyring.

Then, run the Python unittest module:

```
python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
```

If you want to run the tests in an isolated or headless environment, run
this command in a D-Bus session:

```
dbus-run-session -- python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
```

# Get the code

SecretStorage is available under BSD license. The source code can be found
on [GitHub].

[github]: https://github.com/mitya57/secretstorage
[gnome keyring]: https://download.gnome.org/sources/gnome-keyring/
[jeepney]: https://pypi.org/project/jeepney/
[python-cryptography]: https://pypi.org/project/cryptography/
[secret service]: https://specifications.freedesktop.org/secret-service/
[secretstorage.readthedocs.io]: https://secretstorage.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[sphinx]: http://sphinx-doc.org/

modules.name: secretstorage.dhcrypto
documentation:
This module contains needed classes, functions and constants
to implement dh-ietf1024-sha256-aes128-cbc-pkcs7 secret encryption
algorithm.
SecretStorage module for Python
Access passwords using the SecretService DBus API
Author: Dmitry Shachnev, 2014-2018
License: 3-clause BSD, see LICENSE file

modules.name: secretstorage.exceptions
documentation:
All secretstorage functions may raise various exceptions when
something goes wrong. All exceptions derive from base
{exc}`SecretStorageException` class.
SecretStorage module for Python
Access passwords using the SecretService DBus API
Author: Dmitry Shachnev, 2012-2018
License: 3-clause BSD, see LICENSE file

classes.name: secretstorage.exceptions.SecretStorageException
documentation: All exceptions derive from this class.

classes.name: secretstorage.exceptions.SecretServiceNotAvailableException
documentation:
Raised by {class}`~secretstorage.item.Item` or
{class}`~secretstorage.collection.Collection` constructors, or by
other functions in the {mod}`secretstorage.collection` module, when
the Secret Service API is not available.

classes.name: secretstorage.exceptions.LockedException
documentation:
Raised when an action cannot be performed because the collection
is locked. Use {meth}`~secretstorage.collection.Collection.is_locked`
to check if the collection is locked, and
{meth}`~secretstorage.collection.Collection.unlock` to unlock it.

classes.name: secretstorage.exceptions.ItemNotFoundException
documentation:
Raised when an item does not exist or has been deleted. Example of
handling:

>>> import secretstorage
>>> connection = secretstorage.dbus_init()
>>> item_path = '/not/existing/path'
>>> try:
...     item = secretstorage.Item(connection, item_path)
... except secretstorage.ItemNotFoundException:
...     print('Item not found!')
...
Item not found!

classes.name: secretstorage.exceptions.PromptDismissedException
documentation:
Raised when a prompt was dismissed by the user.

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

modules.name: secretstorage
documentation:
This file provides quick access to all SecretStorage API. Please
refer to documentation of individual modules for API details.
SecretStorage module for Python
Access passwords using the SecretService DBus API
Author: Dmitry Shachnev, 2013-2020
License: 3-clause BSD, see LICENSE file

functions.name: secretstorage.dbus_init
documentation:
Returns a new connection to the session bus, instance of
jeepney's {class}`DBusConnection` class. This connection can
then be passed to various SecretStorage functions, such as
{func}`~secretstorage.collection.get_default_collection`.

:::{warning}
The D-Bus socket will not be closed automatically. You can
close it manually using the {meth}`DBusConnection.close` method,
or you can use the {class}`contextlib.closing` context manager:

```python
from contextlib import closing
with closing(dbus_init()) as conn:
    collection = secretstorage.get_default_collection(conn)
    items = collection.search_items({'application': 'myapp'})
```

However, you will not be able to call any methods on the objects
created within the context after you leave it.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 Before the port to Jeepney, this function returned an instance of :class:`dbus.SessionBus` class.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 3.1 This function no longer accepts any arguments.
:::

functions.name: secretstorage.check_service_availability
documentation:
Returns True if the Secret Service daemon is either running or
available for activation via D-Bus, False otherwise.

:::{versionadded} 3.2
:::

modules.name: secretstorage.util
documentation:
This module provides some utility functions, but these shouldn't
normally be used by external applications.
SecretStorage module for Python
Access passwords using the SecretService DBus API
Author: Dmitry Shachnev, 2013-2018
License: 3-clause BSD, see LICENSE file

functions.name: secretstorage.util.open_session
documentation: Returns a new Secret Service session.

functions.name: secretstorage.util.format_secret
documentation:
Formats `secret` to make possible to pass it to the
Secret Service API.

functions.name: secretstorage.util.exec_prompt
documentation:
Executes the prompt in a blocking mode.

:returns: a tuple; the first element is a boolean value showing
          whether the operation was dismissed, the second element
          is a list of unlocked object paths

functions.name: secretstorage.util.unlock_objects
documentation:
Requests unlocking objects specified in `paths`.
Returns a boolean representing whether the operation was dismissed.

:::{versionadded} 2.1.2
:::

functions.name: secretstorage.util.add_match_rules
documentation:
Adds match rules for the given connection.

Currently it matches all messages from the Prompt interface, as the
mock service (unlike GNOME Keyring) does not specify the signal
destination.

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

classes.name: secretstorage.util.DBusAddressWrapper
documentation:
A wrapper class around {class}`jeepney.wrappers.DBusAddress`
that adds some additional methods for calling and working with
properties, and converts error responses to SecretStorage
exceptions.

:::{versionadded} 3.0
:::

modules.name: secretstorage.item
documentation:
SecretStorage item contains a *secret*, some *attributes* and a
*label* visible to user. Editing all these properties and reading the
secret is possible only when the {doc}`collection <collection>` storing
the item is unlocked. The collection can be unlocked using collection's
{meth}`~secretstorage.collection.Collection.unlock` method.
SecretStorage module for Python
Access passwords using the SecretService DBus API
Author: Dmitry Shachnev, 2013-2018
License: 3-clause BSD, see LICENSE file

classes.name: secretstorage.item.Item

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.is_locked
documentation:
Returns {const}`True` if item is locked, otherwise
{const}`False`.

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.ensure_not_locked
documentation:
If collection is locked, raises
{exc}`~secretstorage.exceptions.LockedException`.

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.unlock
documentation:
Requests unlocking the item. Usually, this means that the
whole collection containing this item will be unlocked.

Returns a boolean representing whether the prompt has been
dismissed; that means {const}`False` on successful unlocking
and {const}`True` if it has been dismissed.

:::{versionadded} 2.1.2
:::

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 No longer accepts the ``callback`` argument.
:::

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.get_attributes
documentation: Returns item attributes (dictionary).

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.set_attributes
documentation: Sets item attributes to `attributes` (dictionary).

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.get_label
documentation: Returns item label (unicode string).

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.set_label
documentation: Sets item label to `label`.

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.delete
documentation: Deletes the item.

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.get_secret
documentation: Returns item secret (bytestring).

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.get_secret_content_type
documentation: Returns content type of item secret (string).

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.set_secret
documentation:
Sets item secret to `secret`. If `content_type` is given,
also sets the content type of the secret (`text/plain` by
default).

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.get_created
documentation:
Returns UNIX timestamp (integer) representing the time
when the item was created.

:::{versionadded} 1.1
:::

methods.name: secretstorage.item.Item.get_modified
documentation:
Returns UNIX timestamp (integer) representing the time
when the item was last modified.
Represents a secret item.

modules.name: secretstorage.collection
documentation:
Collection is a place where secret items are stored. Normally, only
the default collection should be used, but this module allows to use any
registered collection. Use {func}`get_default_collection` to get the
default collection (and create it, if necessary).

Collections are usually automatically unlocked when user logs in, but
collections can also be locked and unlocked using
{meth}`Collection.lock` and {meth}`Collection.unlock` methods (unlocking
requires showing the unlocking prompt to user and can be synchronous or
asynchronous). Creating new items and editing existing ones is possible
only in unlocked collection.
SecretStorage module for Python
Access passwords using the SecretService DBus API
Author: Dmitry Shachnev, 2013-2022
License: 3-clause BSD, see LICENSE file

functions.name: secretstorage.collection.create_collection
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`Collection` with the given `label` and `alias`
and returns it. This action requires prompting.

```{eval-rst}

if the prompt is dismissed.
```

functions.name: secretstorage.collection.get_all_collections
documentation: Returns a generator of all available collections.

functions.name: secretstorage.collection.get_default_collection
documentation:
Returns the default collection. If it doesn't exist,
creates it.

functions.name: secretstorage.collection.get_any_collection
secretstorage.collection.get_collection_by_alias
documentation:
Returns the collection with the given `alias`. If there is no
such collection, raises
{exc}`~secretstorage.exceptions.ItemNotFoundException`.

functions.name: secretstorage.collection.search_items
documentation:
Returns a generator of items in all collections with the given
attributes. `attributes` should be a dictionary.

classes.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.is_locked
documentation:
Returns {const}`True` if item is locked, otherwise
{const}`False`.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.ensure_not_locked
documentation:
If collection is locked, raises
{exc}`~secretstorage.exceptions.LockedException`.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.unlock
documentation:
Requests unlocking the collection.

Returns a boolean representing whether the prompt has been
dismissed; that means {const}`False` on successful unlocking
and {const}`True` if it has been dismissed.

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 No longer accepts the ``callback`` argument.
:::

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.lock
documentation: Locks the collection.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.delete
documentation: Deletes the collection and all items inside it.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.get_all_items
documentation: Returns a generator of all items in the collection.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.search_items
documentation:
Returns a generator of items with the given attributes.
`attributes` should be a dictionary.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.get_label
documentation: Returns the collection label.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.set_label
documentation: Sets collection label to `label`.

methods.name: secretstorage.collection.Collection.create_item
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`~secretstorage.item.Item` with given
`label` (unicode string), `attributes` (dictionary) and `secret`
(bytestring). If `replace` is {const}`True`, replaces the existing
item with the same attributes. If `content_type` is given, also
sets the content type of the secret (`text/plain` by default).
Returns the created item.
Represents a collection.
